payload.text:
Coms Clip Design App Prompt

Create a dedicated AI video clipping app named "Coms Clip Design" that transforms long videos into viral shorts, mimicking clip.opus.pro. Use a sleek, modern dark-themed interface (#0a0a0a background, #00ff88 accents, rounded cards) with a vertical mobile-first layout. Break it into clear steps:

Step 1: Upload Video
Top section: Large drag-drop uploader labeled "Upload Long Video (MP4, MOV, up to 60min)". Show progress bar, thumbnail preview, and auto-detected duration/topics.

Step 2: AI Analysis
Below uploader: Auto-trigger analysis button "Analyze with AI". Display loading spinner, then results card with:

"Hook Moments" (top 5-10 clips ranked by virality score).

Key stats: Total clips generated, total duration, estimated views potential.

Sidebar: Topics detected, captions preview, emojis suggested.

Clips Gallery
Scrollable grid of generated shorts (9:16 portrait, 15-60s each). Each clip card shows:

Embedded player (play/pause).

Auto-captions (bold, animated text with emojis).

Virality score badge (e.g., 🔥 92%).

Edit buttons: Trim, reorder, add B-roll, voiceover toggle.

Customization Panel
Right sidebar (collapsible):

Aspect ratios: 9:16 (TikTok/Reels), 16:9 (YouTube), 1:1 (IG).

Styles: Cinematic, Funny, Dramatic, Auto.

Hooks: Add intro text, trending audio sync.

"Regenerate" button for refinements.

Export Section
Bottom: Prominent "Export All Clips" button (batch ZIP download). Per-clip: "Copy to Clipboard", "Share to TikTok/YouTube/IG", "Download MP4". Add "Pro Upgrade" CTA for unlimited exports.

Core Functionality:
On upload, send video to AI (like OpusClip's model): Auto-detect hooks, faces, speech, emotions. Generate 5-20 shorts with dynamic captions, smooth cuts, zoom effects, subtle music/SFX. Natural voice if detected. Preview in real-time. Ensure seamless flow: Upload → Analyze (10-30s) → Edit → Export. Include free tier limits (e.g., 30min/week) with upgrade nag. Responsive for web/mobile, with tutorial tooltip on first use.

**Designing the Core App**

I'm currently focused on the initial React application structure, aiming for a sleek dark mode aesthetic with a #0a0a0a background and #00ff88 accents. The name is "Coms Clip Design," and the central purpose is to facilitate video clipping and "viral short" suggestions via AI, much like `clip.opus.pro`.


**Defining the User Flow**

I've established the key user steps: upload (drag-and-drop), AI analysis, a clips gallery, a customization panel, and export options. I'm focusing on simulating the AI analysis with Gemini, even if actual video file modification in the browser isn't feasible. The stack is React 18, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Lucide Icons, and the Gemini API for the AI features. I'm building out the basic file structure as well.


**Outlining Core Components**

I've mapped out essential components, specifically `ClipGallery`, `CustomizationPanel`, the `gemini.ts` service for AI interactions, and a `types.ts` file for interfaces. I'm focusing on "virality score," captions, duration, and topics derived from the AI analysis as clip attributes. The visual style is solidified with the established dark mode, including background and accent colors. I'm also addressing the file upload state.

payload.text: There was an unexpected error. Finish what you were doing.

**Reviewing Past Implementation**

I'm revisiting the "Coms Clip Design" app project, focusing on the last feedback. The architecture and UI elements appear largely complete. My immediate concern is addressing the reported "unexpected error." I need to understand the source of that error before proceeding.


**Revisiting Original Prompt Goals**

I'm now focusing on the original prompt, given the error message indicates a potential incompleteness. The files are there, so I am going back to the task description to see if the implementation meets the prompt's explicit requirements. I'm verifying that all features requested, such as large video uploads with progress, AI analysis results, and the clips gallery, have been implemented as specified. There may be specific interactivity or features that need revisiting.


**Adding Clip Actions**

I've been examining the detailed per-clip actions specified in the requirements. I need to enhance the ClipCard component to include buttons for individual "Copy to Clipboard", "Share to TikTok/YouTube/IG", and "Download MP4" operations. I'm also preparing a tutorial tooltip for first-time users and refining the "Pro Upgrade" prompts. I'm making sure that the "Regenerate" button works.


**Implementing UI Enhancements**

I'm now implementing the UI refinements. I've focused on the ClipCard component, adding "Copy Link", "Share", and "Download" options, and have also integrated a tutorial overlay for new users. The "Regenerate" button is now functional in the sidebar by connecting it to `handleStartAnalysis`. I am now updating `index.html` to accommodate the CSS for the tooltip, and making sure the tooltip displays correctly. I'm working on the "Pro" badge and the limit tracker, too.

payload.text: remove the paid function and make it run free independently

**Eliminating Paid Features**

I'm removing all traces of paid functionality. That means getting rid of "Go Pro" buttons and any "Pro" badges. References to these paid elements are next on my list. The goal is to make the app entirely self-sufficient and free.

Remove weekly limit indicators from the export bar.

payload.text:
YouTube Link Upload Add-On Prompt

Replace Step 1 in the Coms Clip Design app with this enhanced upload section:

Step 1: Video Source
Create a unified upload card at the top with two seamless options:

File Upload: Large drag-and-drop zone labeled "Upload Long Video File (MP4, MOV, up to 60min)" with progress bar.

YouTube Import (directly below): Text input field labeled "Or paste YouTube link" with placeholder: https://youtube.com/watch?v=abc123.
Add a glowing green button next to input: "Import from YouTube".

YouTube Functionality:
When user pastes valid YouTube URL and clicks "Import from YouTube":

Validate URL format and accessibility

Fetch video metadata via YouTube API (title, thumbnail, duration, channel)

Display preview card below input showing: video thumbnail, title, duration (e.g. "23:45"), and "Ready for clipping ✓"

Store video ID internally as source for AI analysis (no download needed)

Show subtle loading spinner during fetch with error handling for private/unavailable videos

Visual Flow: File upload and YouTube input appear side-by-side on desktop, stacked on mobile. Once either method succeeds, lock the section with green checkmark and show "Video Ready! → Analyze with AI". Both methods feed identical data to Step 2 AI analysis.

**Expanding Upload Options**

I've been working on integrating YouTube upload functionality into Coms Clip Design. Step one, the uploader, is being updated to handle both local file uploads and YouTube imports. I'm focusing on the YouTube aspect: validating the URL and fetching the necessary metadata. For now, the metadata retrieval is mocked, since a real server isn't implemented.


**Refining YouTube Integration**

I'm now diving into the specifics of YouTube integration. I'm focusing on the responsive UI changes for the uploader, building out the section for YouTube input, and implementing the URL validation logic. I'm also planning the new responsive layout (side-by-side on desktop, stacked on mobile). I'm updating the "Ready" state to encompass both local files and YouTube sources. Simultaneously, I'll update the `VideoMetadata` or `AppState` to track YouTube sources. Finally, I'll incorporate the new modern dark theme, responsiveness, interactive elements, and smooth transitions into the code.

entries.description: Add sourceType to VideoMetadata to distinguish between local files and YouTube imports.
Updated handleFileUpload and added handleYouTubeImport to handle the two source types.
Redesigned Step 1 with side-by-side File Upload and YouTube Import, plus a unified preview card.

payload.text: add the option to add captions to the videos with a display list of animated words and word color choice button

**Visualizing Captions Features**

I'm currently focused on the implications of adding captions to videos within the "Coms Clip Design" app. My primary aim is to flesh out the user interface requirements, specifically how users would view and manipulate captions. I'm also starting to think about visually representing the individual words in captions, maybe through some kind of animated list.


**Refining Feature Implementation**

I'm now diving into the implementation details for the "animated words" feature. I've pinpointed specific updates needed for `types.ts`, `services/geminiService.ts`, `components/ClipCard.tsx`, and `components/CustomizationSidebar.tsx`. The goal is to incorporate transcription data and allow users to customize the appearance of the animated words, including a color choice button. I'm focusing on a UI section that will display the transcript word-by-word.

entries.description: Added CaptionWord and CaptionSegment interfaces and updated HookMoment to include transcription data.
Updated Gemini prompt to request detailed word-level transcription for animated captions.
Added captionColor state and passed it to gallery and sidebar components.
Passed captionColor to ClipCard.
Updated ClipCard to use captionColor and added a sophisticated word-by-word caption animation.
Added Caption Color picker and Animated Words list to the sidebar.

payload.text: generate a ai hashtag button option under the generated videos

**Considering AI Hashtag Integration**

I'm currently focused on the implications of adding an "AI Hashtag" button to the "Coms Clip Design" app. Specifically, I'm examining how to integrate this functionality. I've begun by updating the `HookMoment` interface in `types.ts` to accommodate the new button and its associated data flow. I'm prioritizing efficient data handling for the AI-generated hashtags.


**Implementing Hashtag Generation**

I'm now zeroing in on integrating the hashtag generation feature. I plan to add the "AI Hashtags" button directly within `ClipCard.tsx`, deciding whether to display pre-generated hashtags or simulating generation. I'll need to update `analyzeVideoWithAI` or create a new service to manage the hashtag generation logic. The button's behavior will trigger this process, including a loading state and the subsequent display of relevant hashtags.
